name: ghost-align
description: Visually align operators like = without modifying your source code

commands.title: Ghost Align: Disable/Enable for Current Language

ghostAlign.showStatusBar.description: Show a status bar item indicating Ghost Align ON/OFF (click to toggle)

ghostAlign.operators.description: Operators to align (used when the current language is not listed in ghostAlign.operatorsByLanguage). Supported special tokens: "=" (assignment), ":" (JSON/YAML key or CSS declaration), "//" and "#" (trailing line comments), "=>" (arrow function), "\\" (trailing line-continuation marker, e.g. shell/Makefile/C-preprocessor `#define` continuations — a `\` only counts when it is the last non-whitespace character on the line, not one inside a string or escape sequence). For TypeScript/JavaScript, ":" aligns type-annotation / object-literal colons (ternary and string/comment colons are excluded). The "=", "=>", and "\\" tokens exclude occurrences inside strings and comments. Any other string is matched literally, also excluding occurrences inside strings and comments. The list order defines both the priority and the left-to-right column order: each listed operator is aligned as its own column on the same line (e.g. ["=", "#"] aligns assignments and then trailing comments), and a later operator only matches occurrences after the previous column.

ghostAlign.operatorsByLanguage.description: Per-language operator overrides keyed by languageId (e.g. "json", "jsonc", "typescript"). If the current document's language is listed here, this list is used in place of ghostAlign.operators. Each value is a list of operators (see ghostAlign.operators for supported tokens such as "=", ":", "//", "#"); e.g. set "typescript": ["//"] to align trailing comments.

ghostAlign.disabledLanguages.description: Language IDs (e.g. "markdown", "yaml") for which Ghost Align decoration is fully disabled, including Markdown table / CSV / JSDoc alignment. Takes priority over ghostAlign.operatorsByLanguage: listing a language here disables it even if it also has an entry there. Equivalent to setting "<languageId>": [] in ghostAlign.operatorsByLanguage, but discoverable as its own setting.

ghostAlign.jsdoc.enabled.description: Align the parameter-name and description columns of consecutive JSDoc @param lines in JavaScript/TypeScript. Replaces the deprecated ghostAlign.alignJsdocParams, which is still honored while this key is left unset.

ghostAlign.markdownTable.enabled.description: Align Markdown table columns so the | separators line up. Set to false to turn off only the Markdown table alignment; to disable Ghost Align for Markdown entirely, use ghostAlign.disabledLanguages.

ghostAlign.csv.enabled.description: Align CSV/TSV columns so the delimiters line up. Set to false to turn off only the CSV/TSV alignment; to disable Ghost Align for those languages entirely, use ghostAlign.disabledLanguages.

ghostAlign.alignJsdocParams.markdownDeprecationMessage: **Deprecated**: use `#ghostAlign.jsdoc.enabled#` instead. This key is still honored while `ghostAlign.jsdoc.enabled` is left unset.
ghostAlign.alignJsdocParams.description: Align the parameter-name and description columns of consecutive JSDoc @param lines in JavaScript/TypeScript.

ghostAlign.maxPadding.description: Maximum number of ghost characters inserted per alignment column on a single line. For operator and JSDoc @param alignment, when aligning a group would require more, the extremely long outlier lines are excluded and the remaining lines are aligned without them. For Markdown table and CSV/TSV alignment, an outlier cell instead makes its whole column skip alignment (later columns keep aligning from each row's actual position), since excluding a row would leave the table shape broken. 0 means unlimited (default).

ghostAlign.ghostCharacter.description: Character used for ghost padding. Defaults to a non-breaking space (U+00A0), which renders like a half-width space but is not collapsed by the editor's rendering. Plain ASCII spaces will be collapsed to one cell and break alignment — use NBSP or en-space (U+2002) if you customize this. Leaving this empty in the settings UI falls back to the default.

ghostAlign.ghostColor.description: Tint color of the ghost padding (any CSS color string, e.g. "rgba(128,128,128,0.25)", "#88888840", "transparent"). Applied as both foreground and background so whitespace padding is distinguishable from real spaces. Leaving this empty in the settings UI falls back to the default; set "transparent" to disable tinting entirely.

scripts.package:install: npm run package && npm run install:vsix
